question.text: Banks that do not meet the minimum prudential requirements for TM membership can:

answer.text: Participate as clients through other trading members
Not participate in ETCD at all
Only trade in the OTC market

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Banks not meeting TM criteria (net worth 500 Cr, CRAR 10%, NPA 3%, 3-year profit) can still participate as CLIENTS through other TMs. **The Trap:** They're not excluded entirely — they just can't be TMs themselves. **Cross-Exam Context:** Bank participation flexibility is tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This ensures all banks can access ETCD for hedging, even if they can't become direct market members.
